data.title: Nvidia H20 ban could have 'silver lining,' UBS suggests
data.description:
While many analysts believe that Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) needing an indefinite license to export — and essentially being blocked — to sell its H20 GPUs to China is a negative for the Jensen Huang-led company, UBS said it could come with a silver lining. 

Shares fell approximately 6% in premarket trading.

“While clearly reducing NVDA’s ability to beat and raise, this may be a clearing event for the stock if in exchange for committing to build $500B of AI infrastructure in the US over the next [4 years], NVDA used this as concession to effectively kill the AI Diffusion Rule,” analyst Timothy Arcuri wrote in a note to clients.

Earlier this week, a group of Republican senators called on the Trump administration to get rid of the AI Diffusion rule — which was designed to limit the spread of advanced AI technology outside the U.S. — on the grounds that it could impact the U.S.'s leadership in artificial intelligence.

Arcuri, who kept his Buy rating and $185 price target on Nvidia, said completely taking out H20-related revenue would have a $0.20 impact to the company.

In addition, the $5.5B writedown that Nvidia took is “probably” comprised of inventory and the rest in purchase commitments and related reserves.

Assuming $4B in inventory, that would account for roughly $13B in revenue, which Arcuri said is “clearly a significant number.” However, it's unclear how much Nvidia can make up elsewhere, though it is important to note the company did not negatively pre-announce its fiscal first-quarter results and has been adamant that demand is outstripping supply through the rest of 2025.

data.title: Shanghai Electric Group Co., Ltd. reports FY results
data.description:
• Shanghai Electric Group Co., Ltd. press release (OTC:SIELF): FY revenue of RMB 116.19 billion
  • As of December 31, 2024, total assets stood at 302.51 billion yuan

data.title: Sylvamo CEO Jean-Michel Ribieras to retire, John Sims to succeed
data.description:
• Sylvamo (NYSE:SLVM) has announced that Jean-Michel Ribiéras, chairman and CEO, will retire Dec. 31.
  • John Sims, who currently serves as senior vice president and CFO, has been elected COO by the company’s board of directors, effective May 1.
  • As COO, Sims will lead commercial and operational functions, while Ribiéras continues to lead corporate functions until his retirement. Sims will then become Sylvamo’s next CEO Jan 1, 2026.
  • Don Devlin has been elected senior vice president and CFO to succeed Sims, effective May 1.

data.title: Cullinan gets EU nod to begin testing CLN-978 in rheumatoid arthritis
data.description:
• Cullinan Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CGEM) received European Medicines Agency's approval on Wednesday to initiate trial for CLN-978, the company's rheumatoid arthritis treatment candidate.
  • The Phase 1 trial will test CLN-978, a bispecific CD19 T Cell engager administered subcutaneously, in patients with active, difficult-to-treat rheumatoid arthritis.
  • CLN-978 is also being studied for systemic lupus erythematosus in the U.S., Europe, and Australia, the company said.
  • Stock is up 3.7% premarket.

data.title: Stock index futures fall as Nvidia's China licensing requirement fuels trade war concerns
data.description:
Stock index futures were lower on Wednesday, as worries about a U.S.-China trade war rose on news that Nvidia will need a special license to ship graphic processing units to China.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1.7% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.33%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.82%.

The core retail sales beat consensus in March, while the headline number came in line

Wall Street on Tuesday ended slightly lower in a choppy trading session, as a boost from positive bank earnings petered out amid more caution over developments surrounding tariffs.

"After a strong start to the week, the market mood turned more negative again yesterday, as tensions between the US and China showed signs of further escalation," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"So after a brief period of greater stability in markets, that reminded investors about the ongoing risks of escalation, raising fears that the trade war could still get worse from here," Allen added.

Nvidia (NVDA) fell 6.8% in premarket trade after it received a notice from the U.S. government that it needs a special license to ship its H20 graphic processing units or related hardware to China, and such a license will be required indefinitely.

"Some politicians may believe trade is more important than it is. Trade does matter economically, but a 'made in China' product in a store does not mean all the price paid goes to China," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

President Donald Trump has also directed an investigation to determine if U.S. critical minerals imports "threaten to impair national security," which could lead to tariffs or restrictions on the sector.

On the economic side, traders will keep an eye on the March industrial production data, which is expected to come in at -0.2% M/M.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is also expected to speak later in the day.

data.title: ASML falls after bookings miss estimates, CEO warns of uncertainty around US tariffs
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with Evercore Analysts' note.

Shares of ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) fell about 4% premarket on Wednesday after the company warned that tariffs were increasing uncertainty for the long term, but maintained its annual outlook.

Evercore kept its Outperform rating and €965 price target in the stock.

Evercore Analysts led by Mark Lipacis said that ASML stock traded 5% lower in Europe in the morning after first quarter revenues of €7.74B came in 40 basis points, or bps, below Street estimates, €3.9B orders coming in below expectations of €4.8B, and second quarter outlook of €7.45B revenue missing by 4%, and gross margin outlook of 50% and 53% also below, amid 100 bps tariff related headwinds.

Tariffs and Outlook: 

"There's this new uncertainty around tariffs. And like many experts, many businesses are explaining this is, of course, something that we don't know how to quantify yet. But this is adding definitely uncertainty on the long term," said CEO Christophe Fouquet in a video uploaded on ASML's website.

However, Fouquet said that they see a lot of strength in AI. "In fact some of the demand for this year, of course, but also for next year has solidified. So that's very encouraging,"  Fouquet noted.

Fouquet noted that, adding to this, the discussion the company had with its customers, points to 2025 and 2026 to be both growth years.

ASML's CFO Roger Dassen broke down four categories by which tariffs could impact the company. These were shipments to the U.S.; tariffs on imported parts and tools; material imports required for its manufacturing in the U.S.; and tariffs from other countries on U.S. exports.

"We're very actively working with the entire ecosystem to try and minimize the overall impact on the whole ecosystem as a result of that," said Dassen.

Dassen noted that it was too early to discuss what extent of impact tariffs would have on global gross domestic product, or GDP, and total market demand, "and therefore it's quite impossible actually to put a number on that."

Revenue Outlook:

ASML kept its 2025 total net sales outlook unchanged, and expects it to between €30B and €35B (with mid-point at €32.5B).

ASML expects second quarter 2025 total net sales to be between €7.2B and €7.7B (with mid-point at €7.45B).

CEO Fouquet said in the earnings release, "artificial intelligence continues to be the primary growth driver in our industry. It has created a shift in the market dynamics that benefits some customers more than others, contributing to both upside potential and downside risks as reflected in our 2025 revenue range."

Key Metrics:

The Dutch company — which makes extreme ultraviolet lithography, or EUV, machines — saw first quarter of 2025 total net sales grow about 46.3% year-over-year to €7.74B, which missed estimates. Compared to the fourth quarter of 2024, net sales fell 16.4%.

Bookings:

The company reported bookings of €3.94B billion, which was less than the average estimate of €4.82B by analysts, according to a report by Bloomberg News. Net bookings for the EUV machines in the first quarter were €1.2B.

While reporting its fourth quarter results in January, ASML had said that it would stop reporting order bookings after 2025.

China Sales:

China accounted for 27% of the total net system sales of about €5.742B, behind the South Korea with 40% share in the first quarter of 2025. U.S. and Taiwan had 16% share, each in the first quarter of 2025, according to the company's investor presentation.

In the fourth quarter of 2024, China accounted for 27% of the total net system sales of about €7.12B. Fouquet had said then that in 2025 they would see China going back to a more normal ratio in their business.

The U.S. has placed several export restrictions on companies, including ASML, to curb China's access to advanced semiconductor equipment, which, among other things, is used in making AI products. 

Dividend: ASML intends to declare a total dividend of €6.40 per ordinary share for 2024. Recognizing the three interim dividends of €1.52 per ordinary share paid in 2024 and 2025, this leads to a final dividend proposal of €1.84 per ordinary share.

Stock Buyback: ASML said that in the first quarter, it bought around 4M shares for about €2.7B, under the current 2022-2025 share buyback program.

data.title: Charles Schwab makes minority investment in estate planning platform Wealth.com
data.description:
With the goal of enhancing its wealth management offering, Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) made a minority investment in AI-powered estate planning platform Wealth.com, it said on Wednesday.

Wealth.com enables financial advisors to help clients optimize their estate plans or give clients without an estate plan the ability to immediately self-create robust legal documents (e.g., wills and revocable trusts) in all 50 U.S. states and D.C. 

Schwab's (NYSE:SCHW) investment is set to make this process easier.

"Wealth.com is an important first step in building out a support ecosystem for our advisor clients as they respond to investors’ needs, while also providing a scalable and easy-to-use solution for our retail clients to meet more of their financial needs at Charles Schwab,” Charles Schwab President and CEO Rick Wurster said in a statement.

As an extension of the investment, the two companies are also developing opportunities to offer access to Wealth.com’s estate planning tools to Schwab’s (SCHW) clients. Additional information and launch details will be shared at a later date.

Terms of the investment have not been disclosed.

SCHW shares slipped 0.3% in premarket trading.

data.title: SA Charts: Semiconductor sales dip in Feb sequentially, Americas falls most among regions
data.description:
Worldwide semiconductor sales were $54.9 billion during the month of February, noting a sequential decline for the second straight month. 

February sales decreased 2.9% from the previous month but rose 17.1% compared to the same period last year, the World Semiconductor Trade Statistics data said. 

The Americas, which account for nearly half of the semiconductor sales, saw the biggest month-on-month dip in sales at 4.6%, to $18.64B in February. 

 

 

As the Trump administration continues to tweak its tariff policies, the Department of Commerce is now looking into the potential impact of semiconductor imports on national security, likely setting the stage for tariffs on the sectors.

Semiconductor sales in China and Japan noted a 3.1% sequential decline in February. Sales in Asia Pacific held steady versus January. 

Compared to last year, the Americas recorded a 48.4% jump in semiconductor sales, while Asia Pacific was the runner-up at a 10.8% growth pace. China saw a 5.6% year-on-year rise in sales in February.
